url.doc: A URL to a TileJSON resource. Supported protocols are `http:` and `https:`.

tiles.doc: An array of one or more tile source URLs, as in the TileJSON spec.

bounds.doc: An array containing the longitude and latitude of the southwest and northeast corners of the source's bounding box in the following order: `[sw.lng, sw.lat, ne.lng, ne.lat]`. When this property is included in a source, no tiles outside of the given bounds are requested by MapLibre.

scheme.doc: Influences the y direction of the tile coordinates. The global-mercator (aka Spherical Mercator) profile is assumed.

minzoom.doc: Minimum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec.

maxzoom.doc: Maximum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec. Data from tiles at the maxzoom are used when displaying the map at higher zoom levels.

attribution.doc: Contains an attribution to be displayed when the map is shown to a user.

promoteId.doc: A property to use as a feature id (for feature state). Either a property name, or an object of the form `{<sourceLayer>: <propertyName>}`. If specified as a string for a vector tile source, the same property is used across all its source layers.

volatile.doc: A setting to determine whether a source's tiles are cached locally.

mvt.doc: Mapbox Vector Tiles. See http://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec for more info.

mlt.doc: MapLibre Vector Tiles. See https://github.com/maplibre/maplibre-tile-spec for more info.

encoding.doc: The encoding used by this source. Mapbox Vector Tiles encoding is used by default.

*.doc: Other keys to configure the data source.

url.doc: A URL to a TileJSON resource. Supported protocols are `http:` and `https:`.

tiles.doc: An array of one or more tile source URLs, as in the TileJSON spec.

bounds.doc: An array containing the longitude and latitude of the southwest and northeast corners of the source's bounding box in the following order: `[sw.lng, sw.lat, ne.lng, ne.lat]`. When this property is included in a source, no tiles outside of the given bounds are requested by MapLibre.

minzoom.doc: Minimum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec.

maxzoom.doc: Maximum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec. Data from tiles at the maxzoom are used when displaying the map at higher zoom levels.

tileSize.doc: The minimum visual size to display tiles for this layer. Only configurable for raster layers.

scheme.doc: Influences the y direction of the tile coordinates. The global-mercator (aka Spherical Mercator) profile is assumed.

attribution.doc: Contains an attribution to be displayed when the map is shown to a user.

volatile.doc: A setting to determine whether a source's tiles are cached locally.

*.doc: Other keys to configure the data source.

url.doc: A URL to a TileJSON resource. Supported protocols are `http:` and `https:`.

tiles.doc: An array of one or more tile source URLs, as in the TileJSON spec.

bounds.doc: An array containing the longitude and latitude of the southwest and northeast corners of the source's bounding box in the following order: `[sw.lng, sw.lat, ne.lng, ne.lat]`. When this property is included in a source, no tiles outside of the given bounds are requested by MapLibre.

minzoom.doc: Minimum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec.

maxzoom.doc: Maximum zoom level for which tiles are available, as in the TileJSON spec. Data from tiles at the maxzoom are used when displaying the map at higher zoom levels.

tileSize.doc: The minimum visual size to display tiles for this layer. Only configurable for raster layers.

attribution.doc: Contains an attribution to be displayed when the map is shown to a user.

terrarium.doc: Terrarium format PNG tiles. See https://aws.amazon.com/es/public-datasets/terrain/ for more info.

mapbox.doc: Mapbox Terrain RGB tiles. See https://www.mapbox.com/help/access-elevation-data/#mapbox-terrain-rgb for more info.

custom.doc: Decodes tiles using the redFactor, blueFactor, greenFactor, baseShift parameters.

encoding.doc: The encoding used by this source. Mapbox Terrain RGB is used by default.

redFactor.doc: Value that will be multiplied by the red channel value when decoding. Only used on custom encodings.

blueFactor.doc: Value that will be multiplied by the blue channel value when decoding. Only used on custom encodings.

greenFactor.doc: Value that will be multiplied by the green channel value when decoding. Only used on custom encodings.

baseShift.doc: Value that will be added to the encoding mix when decoding. Only used on custom encodings.

volatile.doc: A setting to determine whether a source's tiles are cached locally.

*.doc: Other keys to configure the data source.

type.doc: The data type of the GeoJSON source.

data.doc: A URL to a GeoJSON file, or inline GeoJSON.

maxzoom.doc: Maximum zoom level at which to create vector tiles (higher means greater detail at high zoom levels).

attribution.doc: Contains an attribution to be displayed when the map is shown to a user.

buffer.doc: Size of the tile buffer on each side. A value of 0 produces no buffer. A value of 512 produces a buffer as wide as the tile itself. Larger values produce fewer rendering artifacts near tile edges and slower performance.

filter.doc: An expression for filtering features prior to processing them for rendering.

tolerance.doc: Douglas-Peucker simplification tolerance (higher means simpler geometries and faster performance).

cluster.doc:
If the data is a collection of point features, setting this to true clusters the points by radius into groups. Cluster groups become new `Point` features in the source with additional properties:

 * `cluster` Is `true` if the point is a cluster 

 * `cluster_id` A unique id for the cluster to be used in conjunction with the [cluster inspection methods](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-gl-js/docs/API/classes/GeoJSONSource/#getclusterexpansionzoom)

 * `point_count` Number of original points grouped into this cluster

 * `point_count_abbreviated` An abbreviated point count

clusterRadius.doc: Radius of each cluster if clustering is enabled. A value of 512 indicates a radius equal to the width of a tile.

clusterMaxZoom.doc: Max zoom on which to cluster points if clustering is enabled. Defaults to one zoom less than maxzoom (so that last zoom features are not clustered). Clusters are re-evaluated at integer zoom levels so setting clusterMaxZoom to 14 means the clusters will be displayed until z15.

clusterMinPoints.doc: Minimum number of points necessary to form a cluster if clustering is enabled. Defaults to `2`.

clusterProperties.doc:
An object defining custom properties on the generated clusters if clustering is enabled, aggregating values from clustered points. Has the form `{"property_name": [operator, map_expression]}`. `operator` is any expression function that accepts at least 2 operands (e.g. `"+"` or `"max"`) - it accumulates the property value from clusters/points the cluster contains; `map_expression` produces the value of a single point.

Example: `{"sum": ["+", ["get", "scalerank"]]}`.

For more advanced use cases, in place of `operator`, you can use a custom reduce expression that references a special `["accumulated"]` value, e.g.:

`{"sum": [["+", ["accumulated"], ["get", "sum"]], ["get", "scalerank"]]}`

lineMetrics.doc: Whether to calculate line distance metrics. This is required for line layers that specify `line-gradient` values.

generateId.doc: Whether to generate ids for the geojson features. When enabled, the `feature.id` property will be auto assigned based on its index in the `features` array, over-writing any previous values.

promoteId.doc: A property to use as a feature id (for feature state). Either a property name, or an object of the form `{<sourceLayer>: <propertyName>}`.

type.doc: The data type of the image source.

url.doc: URL that points to an image.

coordinates.doc: Corners of image specified in longitude, latitude pairs.

type.doc: The data type of the video source.

urls.doc: URLs to video content in order of preferred format.

coordinates.doc: Corners of video specified in longitude, latitude pairs.
